response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist web/backend engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) but does not match the core requirements of this role. C++ exposure is minimal and dated — only a brief stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments (2014-2015) plus a university thesis, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++. There is no evidence of Windows platform development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB), and no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain work (RIDE Capital was a Node.js/GraphQL fintech product, not capital-markets C++ systems). The mandatory technical pillars are essentially absent.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering experience with strong TDD and CI/CD craftsmanship
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a low-level audio thesis project
Excellent communication shown through talks, workshops, and course authoring
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years stale; far short of the required 5-10 years hands-on
No SQL Server/Oracle and no Windows-platform development background
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain
